title: cerastium pumilum subsp. obscurum
intro: Cerastium pumilum subsp. obscurum, commonly known as the alpine chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of alpine chickweed include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting wound healing and soothing skin irritations. Alpine chickweed can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically as a salve or ointment to apply directly to affected areas.
